questions.question: What is the difference between REST and GraphQL?
questions.answer: REST: multiple endpoints, fixed data structures, uses HTTP verbs. GraphQL: single endpoint, client specifies exact data needs, avoids over/under-fetching.
questions.explanation: REST is simpler and more cacheable. GraphQL excels when clients need varying subsets of data. GraphQL reduces round-trips but adds complexity with schema definitions and resolvers.

questions.question: Explain database indexing. When should you use it?
questions.answer: Indexes speed up data retrieval by creating sorted data structures (B-trees). Use for: columns in WHERE clauses, JOIN conditions, ORDER BY columns. Trade-off: faster reads, slower writes, more storage.
questions.explanation: B-tree indexes provide O(log n) lookups. Covering indexes include all query columns, avoiding table lookups. Partial indexes on filtered subsets can be more efficient.
